feed.title: SAP Community - SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
feed.subtitle: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to enable proxy management feature
entry.content:
Login in instance > Navigate to proxy management >Scroll down > Enable Advanced proxy management (Proxy now and proxy import) > Will get Proxy now feature in home page
Another way
Go to Settings > Click on proxy > Click on Become proxy >Proxy any username

entry.title: After the new release, employees cannot reach their employee profile
entry.content:
Hi,

On saturday the new release had been launched by the SAP. On sunday, I did some changes and the system worked properly. 

However, today morning, around 9 am CET, my customer let me know that they cannot reach the employee profiles. 

Around 8 am CET, somebody said that he was able to reach it so it is really confusing. 

Now, including me, who has superadmin access, so I should see everything, I see just the same as the other employees.

 

Do you have any information about SuccessFactors Ouateges?

 

Thank you,

Norbert

entry.title: "Internal error; contact system administrator" when trying to send email to imported employees IAS
entry.content: This error message shows when we try to send emails to the imported users on the cloud identity services platform. Any help please?

entry.title: My customer's employee cannot log in into SuccessFactors AGAIN!
entry.content:
Hi,

After the yesterday morning ouatege, my customer cannot log in into the SuccessFactors system again, due to invalid login. I also cannot log in into the instance nor the test system or to the IAS. Do you have any updates or news with this from other countires?

Thank you!

Norbert

entry.title: Failing the SAP SuccessFactors System-Based Certification Exam Despite Correct Task Execution
entry.content:
Hello,

I’ve taken the Configuring SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Core and Position Management system-based certification exam twice now, and unfortunately I’m still failing it. What’s confusing to me is that during the exam, most of my configurations and tasks seem to work exactly as requested in the objectives. The execution and results appear correct, so I’m struggling to understand where I’m losing points.

One thing I’m especially unsure about is the handling of data models and DTD files during the exam. In the exam environment, the DTD files are not provided to me when working with data models. Should I ignore this fact during the exam, or is there a way we’re expected to obtain those files within the system?

For those who have passed system-based SAP exams before:

  • Are there common mistakes that are overlooked even when the task appears to work?

  • Does SAP grade based on specific configuration methods rather than just the final result?

  • How strict is the validation/grading process?

  • Are there hidden requirements or validation points that are easy to miss?

I’d really appreciate any advice or insight from people who’ve gone through this experience. I’m trying to understand what I might be doing wrong before attempting the exam again.

Thank you.

entry.title: SAP Improvement - Improve Purge Preview Report and Complete Report input data of DRTM Workflow Purge
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have a new improvement raised during March 2026 in SAP Customer Influence. See the link below:

Request ID: 369284 - Improve Purge Preview Report and Complete Report input data of DRTM Workflow Purge Type

We raised it due to currently in SAP SF the "DRTM Workflow Purge type" when checked the preview and complete report input data is not sufficient information to validate which workflows related to the employee are being removed. Only the summed amount.

Our request is to improvement of the input data with further information.

 


For example an entry per each workflow the person will get/have purged with:
Workflow ID;
Workflow Name.

This way it will be easier to understand the report and information that it will be removed from the user.

Thanks in advance for your support.

Cláudia Velez

entry.title: SAP Successfactors Employee Central - RBP Optimization
entry.content:
Role-Based Permissions (RBP) is a security model that allows you to restrict and grant access to your SAP SuccessFactors HCM suite. RBP controls access to the applications that employees can see and edit.

There’re four concepts regarding RBP:

  • Permission role: Define what actions a user can perform and what data they can access e.g., approving timesheets, or editing compensation
  • Granted population: Define who gets the permission e.g., all HR Managers
  • Target population: Determine whose data the Permission Group can act upon e.g., a manager's target population is their direct reports
  • Role assignment: A relationship between a permission role and its access population and target population

Steps for RBP Optimization:

  • Review Overlapping Permissions in Roles.
  • Review and remove group with zero members
  • Remove Inactive Roles
  • RBP Reports

Recommendations for RBP Optimization:

  • More use of Dynamic Groups than Static Groups
  • Reduce the number of Permission Groups
  • Reduce the number of RBP Administrator
  • Naming Conventions for Roles and Groups
  • Use RBP Check Tool
  • RBP Troubleshooting Tool

Overlapping Permissions in Roles

Different employees have the same permissions granted several times in different roles assigned that means when an employee wants to use a functionality, the system validates the permissions granted to that functionality in each role granted to the employee and for the different target populations defined, which incurring performance issues.

Recommendations

  • The focus when creating roles and groups should be the permission and not the employee means roles should be defined based on the set of functionalities that are required to perform an activity and then grant that role to the necessary users.
  • Review the Check Tool and investigate roles that duplicate permissions for the specified user in the exported results.

Use of Dynamic Groups than Static Groups

Use of Dynamic groups will add the employee to the required granted group automatically instead of manually adding the employee, also increases system performance.

Choosing the required people pool criteria or custom flags helps in creating the dynamic groups.

Remove Redundant Groups and Roles

Remove the groups with zero active employees and inactive roles to streamline maintenance and help to avoid system performance issues.

Reduce the number of RBP Administrator

Having many people involved in updating and maintaining RBP can cause security issues or user errors if not communicated and documented appropriately.

Maintain RBP workbook to track and maintain the RBP provides a quick reference for group and role design, helps to avoid overlapping configuration, and is a good resource when planning updates or overhauls of how your configuration is done.

RBP Naming Convention

Meaningful group and role names and role descriptions help customers to identify the correct groups and roles later during maintenance and troubleshooting.

Not naming your groups indicating whether they are Granted or Target can lead to a lack of understanding on what the Groups do and to duplicity of the same.

Example: GG_HRAdmin & TG_HRAdmin

RBP Reports

Reports help to troubleshoot and understand the permissions that have been configured. RBP Table reports are an aggregate of all the RBP data in your system.

KBA - https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-platform/implementing-role-based-permissions/rbp-table-reports

Use of Check Tool

The Check Tool can check your configuration and identify any potential problem areas, and recommend steps to resolve those issues, with or without submitting a support ticket. It’s beneficial to run RBP checks after:

  • You make a major set of changes to RBP.
  • You complete an instance refresh.
  • You use Instance Sync.

KBA - Using the Check Tool to Solve Issues | SAP Help Portal

RBP Troubleshooting Tool

We can use RBP Troubleshooting to better prevent, diagnose, and fix RBP issues.

Details of the RBP Troubleshooting:

  • User Role and Permission Search allow you to search for and compare permission roles and user permissions. You're able to search for the roles and permissions of a single user. Alternatively, you can search for and compare the roles and permissions of two users.
  • Target Population Search allows you to search for the target populations that an employee user can access or manage based on specific permissions.
  • Compare Roles allows you to search for permissions of a role or compare the permissions of two roles.
  • User Group Search allows you to search the permission groups that the user belongs to. It includes the following information: group ID, group name, user type, and group type.

Benefits:

  • Increases system performance
  • Avoid Redundancy
  • Better governance
  • Reduces maintenance of Permission roles and groups 
  • Security
  • Limited Roles and Groups

entry.title: Identifying integration systems details using SFAPI Audit Log
entry.content: I have noticed that there are logs generated in the SFAPI Audit Log screen in SAP SuccessFactors system. These logs are using the user id's PTP_SFAPI and IAM_SFAPI. When checked, there is very little information on which systems are connecting to SAP SuccessFactors using the SFAPI method. As SAP has deprecated the SFAPI, I need help to identify which system is connecting to SAP SuccessFactors and then move the integration to modern methods. Please suggest how I can identify which system is connecting to SAP SuccessFactors that is responsible for these logs in SFAPI Audit Log screen.

entry.title: How to trigger the i9 reverification for the employees who are hired directly in Employee Central
entry.content: We have certain employees whose profiles were migrated from another system to SuccessFactors. For these employees, the I-9 tasks (Section 1 and Section 2) were completed offline outside SuccessFactors through email. Now we need to complete the I-9 reverification for these employees. Please advise on how this can be handled.

entry.title: SmartRecruiters + EC Position Management best practices?
entry.content:
Hello to the community

I’m currently working on a project where we’re looking at SmartRecruiters in combination with SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central (with Position Management enabled), and I’d be really interested in hearing how others have approached a few topics.

We’re facing some questions on process alignment, especially coming from a fairly structured SAP mindset.

First topic is around job creation vs positions. In standard SR it is possible to create jobs independently from EC positions, which is quite different from how some organizations want to operate . Have some of you implemented governance or configurations to enforce (or at least strongly encourage) job creation based on EC positions?

Also, have you seen any implementations where a position could be created in EC when a job is created in SmartRecruiters, or is this always handled outside of standard integrations?

About the hiring team setup, formore dynamic scenarios (e.g. assigning recruiters based on BU / location / job attributes), how far can we realistically go ? as far as I know, access groups is a way to give access to jobs meeting specific conditions but it is same level of access for everyone in the access group, no differentiation between recruiter and manager...Is there another way to do that? Through custom integration of some sort?

Also one challenge we face is that SmartRecruiters manages both the recruitment request and the job posting as a single object/workflow, whereas some organizations are used to a stricter separation (manager creates request → HR reviews → then job is published). Have you found good ways to structure this in SmartRecruiters (e.g. using approvals, draft stages, restricted publication), or is it more about adapting the process?

Any feedback, lessons learned, or design patterns would be super helpful.

Thanks so much

entry.title: Workflow not triggering
entry.content: i created this rule, when selecting the event and event reason as promotion.... and when saving the record in employee profile, its not triggering the workflow WF_EC_PROMOTION.

entry.title: How to make Matrix manager not Mandadory on the position
entry.content: Matrix manager field on position is mandadory and not all our positions should be mandadory, how do we remove that

entry.title: Workflow approval history no longer visible
entry.content:
Hi,
After moving to the latest people profile and updating permission as needed, our compensation team no longer can view workflow approval history. Legacy people profile they were able to see it with no issue. 

Is anyone else encountering this issue as well? We've given all the permissions that is required and it's not working. 

Any advice or guidance is much appreciated! 

Thanks in advance!
Annie

entry.title: How to calculate Annualized Salary Pay Component from Biweekly and other Pay Component Round it up?
entry.content:
The standard pay component group calculation is not rounding values accurately. Therefore, we intend to request the client to consolidate biweekly and other relevant pay components into a separate pay component (e.g., Annual Salary), apply the required rounding, and then include the result in a pay component group.

We would like to confirm whether this approach is feasible and if any reference materials can be provided to support it.

For context, the client is currently operating on S/4 and is transitioning to SuccessFactors, where two different calculation methods are used based on 26 and 26.1 payroll periods.

Note: Creating Custom Annualization Factors of 26.1 is also leading to Rounding issues/

entry.title: Configure a "Display Note" to approvers (Leaders) on the workflow in Employee central
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are creating a Workflow for termination and we would want the the leaders to be able to see a display note that asks them to review a few items/checklist before approving the workflow . Could anyone please let me know if there is such a functionality in our current workflow set up in Employee central?

entry.title: Trigger Notification / Alert based on workflow approval / rejection
entry.content:
Hi,

Is it possible, using business rules, to trigger notifications/alerts based on the status (approved/rejected) of a workflow?

The workflow goes to the line manager two months before the contract end date. If the manager approves the workflow, a notification should be sent to extend the contract. If the manager approves the workflow, a notification should be sent that the contract will not be extended.

entry.title: Story Reports - Creating a change audit report as a story report - Compare effective-dated records
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have created a report to show changes in compensation data for a single employee at a time. I am trying to "filter out" records that don't show changes in the specific pay components I'm looking at.

Current configuration:

The bar charts successfully shows the data, but since my data model comes from Compensation Information, it creates a new data point for every record in the portlet, even if the specific pay components I'm using have not changed.


So the bars are for records in Dec 2023, Jan 2024, Dec 2024,  Jan 2025 and Dec 2025. You can see that the second and third values are the same, as well as the fourth and fifth. That's because something was changed in the compensation portlet (= new record), only not the pay components I'm using. 

For clarification: Every copy of the compensation item is filtered to a specific pay component. Some columns in my table in the report are calculations using these components.

So again: I don't want to show a bar if the record does not present changes in the specific pay components I'm looking at. 

Is there a way to compare each record to its previous record?

I have found this post: https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-members/successfactors-people-analytics-stories-how-to-create-change-report-current/ba-p/13955924 

However, using this method one can only compare the latest record to its previous, not iteratively each record to their respective previous one.

Thanks in advance!
Ana

entry.title: Successfactors emplo
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am currently troubleshooting a new hire import issue in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central.

I imported new employees using the New Employee Import template and completed only the initial/basic employee import successfully. At this stage, I intentionally stopped before proceeding with additional imports such as:

  • Job Information
  • Employment Information
  • Compensation Information
  • Email Information
    etc.

I wanted to first troubleshoot and validate the initial employee creation before moving further.

Current issue:
After the basic import, I can see related records such as job codes, but when searching employees in SuccessFactors, the employee names themselves are not appearing correctly in search results.

In some cases:

  • the employee ID exists,
  • the job code appears,
  • but the employee profile/name is not searchable as expected.

I am trying to understand:

  1. Is this expected behavior if only the basic import is completed?
  2. Are there specific entities/imports required before the employee becomes fully searchable in EC?
  3. Could this be related to missing User ID / Person ID External / Employment linkage?
  4. Is there a recommended sequence for completing new hire imports in EC to avoid partial employee creation issues?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Create a Welcome email when a new employee is hired with time schedule to send after 24h after hired
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Is it possible to create a Welcome email when a new employee is hired format the message text and set a time parameter so that it is sent 24 hours after the employee is added to the system?


Employee Central - SuccessFactors doubts

Kind regards,
Miss Jo

entry.title: SF_COM_Compa_Ratio_and_Range_Penetration_v2.0 merges compa ratio from multiple employee assignments
entry.content:
Hello,

The named SAP template provides a merged result of compa ratio from multiple assignments (both active & inactive) employee has.
Employment Status filter in Query or in report page does not allow to filter out inactive employees (that would include inactive assignments as well). Using that filter results in timing out the session in browser or making an error and not allowing to save the filter setting.
If anyone has experienced similar situation, how did you resolve it?

Tadas
